svg.title: <size:18>Online Auction System with Mediator Pattern (Annotated for Interview)</size>

g.text: Online Auction System with Mediator Pattern (Annotated for Interview)
Trace: "Seller lists a vintage watch. 3 bidders place bids. Auction mediator coordinates: validates, broadcasts, tracks highest bid, ends auction, notifies winner."
bidders, and the auction itself all need to
ALL communication goes through one hub.
Participants never talk to each other directly.
Bidder doesn't know about other bidders.
notifies outbid bidder, notifies seller, broadcasts
new highest bid. ONE method, all coordination."
. They hold a reference to the
Mediator but NEVER to each other."
Bidder doesn't know who the seller is.
calls this to push events to participants."
is the central object: item, seller,
bids list, current highest bid, start/end time,
reserve price (minimum to sell), and state."
records: who bid, how much, when,
and whether it was an auto-bid."
Pattern: Chain of Responsibility | OCP, SRP
Active? Yes. Self-bid? No. Eligible? Yes.
c. Broadcast to all watchers: 'New high bid!'
about Bidder2, Seller, watchers... tight coupling.
OUTBID: notify the outbid bidder specifically.
"Seller lists vintage watch via Mediator. 3 bidders
place bids — all through AuctionHouse (Mediator). Bidder1 bids 5000.
notifies outbid Bidder2 ('You've been outbid!'), notifies Seller
Auction timer expires -> Mediator ends auction, announces winner,
Without it, N bidders + seller + watchers =
N*(N+1) direct connections. With Mediator: everyone talks to
ONE hub. Add a new participant type (Auctioneer)? Register with
AuctionHouse coordinates ALL communication between participants
Seller lists item, Bidder places bid — both via Mediator only
Auction: item, bids, highest bid, reserve, start/end time
Active? Not self-bid? Eligible? Min increment met?
Mediator.placeBid(): validate -> update -> notify ALL parties
NEW_BID / OUTBID / WON / CANCELLED events via Email/Push/SMS
: Mediator = hub. Bidders/Sellers = spokes. No spoke-to-spoke communication.
